title: Level 8.5
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:ㅤ}}__NOTOC__ =
Level 8.5
= {{Underline|FFFFFF}}
The Vantage Point
(Featuring: "Echoes", "A Point of Returning", and "Disadvantage Point")
{{Audio|file=Aphex twin stone in focus slowed reverb.mp3|align=center|=no|volume=0.5}} {{Class Variable|entity=Varying Environmental Factors}} {{Blurimage|file=Disoa.jpg|caption=Image of Level 8.5|align=center|width=500px}} ==
Description
== '''Level 8.5''' is a complex cave system of linear turns made from pure potassium metal. The start of the level, coined as the spawn point of the level, consists of a long 100-meter tunnel at the beginning containing perfluorocarbon liquid on the floor, resembling the appearance of water. One can also spot redwood debris, along with a paifang entering into the main level. According to LBM readings, there is no humidity or water vapour present at all in the level. Bringing water into the level, or items containing it will cause the water particles to disappear. Ice, however, does not disappear, but turns into Ice VII, then sublimates into normal air. '''Level 8.5''' comprises 3 seasons, the cycle spanning 2 years. The seasons consist of phenomena exclusive to each season (which will be described in the phenomena section of the document). Wet, Thermótita, Dry. The wet season is described as the coldest out of the 3, consisting of temperatures ranging from -5 to 10 Celsius from months January to April. Due to the abundance of perfluorocarbon liquid appearance during this season, it is coined the wet season. The Thermótita season is known to be the hottest season. Temperatures range from 4 to 25 Celsius and span from May to July (the next year). The dry season, as seen by its name, is known to be the opposite of the wet season, and is known to be lacking in perfluorocarbon liquid appearances. Temperatures range from 7 to 15 Celsius. Rarely, one can find small dim yellow lanterns. Notably, these lanterns are lit up anomalously, only lighting up when a wanderer is nearby. One shoul be cautious around these lanterns, as these lanterns contain water in the middle concealed by glass as a way to fuel the fire presumably. If this glass breaks and the water spills onto the cave, the potassium will start to combust until the water has dissipated (this is due to the potassium being extremely combustive with water). More than 10 minutes of exposure to light sources (excluding lanterns) has been observed to cause numerous negative effects simultaneously. Immediately after the 10-minute mark, one can notice symptoms of fever and dehydration. Aftereffects typically last from 3 hours to 12 hours. Aftereffects include vitamin K drainage, which causes slower clotting factors, causing significant bleeding to open wounds. One may also notice more weakness in their bones at the same time. One must turn off their devices' Bluetooth or Back Local Wifi, as electromagnetic radiation has been observed to be harmful to wanderers, long-term exposure (30 minutes to an hour) to it causes nerve impulses, brief moments of weakness or discomfort, and random production of melatonin (a chemical responsible for your brains sleep response). If one leaves out edibles, or food for longer than 3 days to a week, the food internally grows the mineral magnesium. Magnesium is recognised to be one of the most essential minerals for the body, also being one of the most abundant, however, if the food is left out 5 days after it starts growing magnesium, it will start gradually growing faster, in 3 weeks, the growing will stop and the mineral will be visually visible. Eating food wherein the magnesium is visually present is extremely dangerous, causing a heavy dose of magnesium into your system will cause extreme fatigue and low blood pressure, however, in some individuals, paralysis and hyperventilation, then will likely cause comas or cardiac arrest afterwards. Tunnels that typically flood with perfluorocarbon fluid exhibit the right environment for the growth of the 3 exclusive plants found in this level, Jarsenoscossis, Panstuansis, and Cavobs. These plants are all characterised to be huge, abnormally coloured, and work for the levels ecosystem in peculiar ways. Jarsenoscossis, the most common plant that can grow in any perfluorocarbon liquid environment, typically resembles a 10-meter tall, 1-meter wide, mixed with brown, red, and green, with a wood-resembling stem. The plant, in its initial stage, starts when bacteria buildup starts in the water, eventually creating a sapling (resembling a leaf), in 2 days, the sapling will grow into stage 2, where the plant will start getting wider. In 5 days, the plant will move into stage 3, the last stage. Panstuansis is known to be the smallest of the 3, however, being one of the simplest and fast-growing ones. The plant consists of 2 stages. Stage 1: if potassium ends up in the perfluorocarbon fluid, the plant will start to develop a base, in 2 hours, becoming a sapling. In 2 days, after it has developed its roots and stem, it moves onto stage 2, the mature stage. The plant will grow to 2 meters tall and 1 meter wide and will consist of micro leaves that produce small amounts of [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. Cavos is the rarest, longest living, and essential plant for the level, known for producing oxygen and sometimes other plants. Cavos is typically found in environments where there is enough Almond Water in the perfluorocarbon fluid, causing the Almond Water to clump together, turn solid, and become the seed of a Cavos plant. Cavos comprises 5 stages. Stage 1 lasts for a month and consists of the plant growing a base and roots; stage 2 lasts for 3 months and involves the plant taking in the potassium metal as nutrients, forming the leaves and the colour. Stage 3 lasts for 2 weeks and the plant rapidly grows taller, and the color has finally stopped changing. Stage 4 lasts for 3 weeks and in this stage, the plant starts growing micro leaves, the stem gets thicker, produces oxygen, and the leaves start dropping, giving potential to other new plants. To hydrate within the level due to a severe lack of water, one must generally rely on [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. However, if you add salt or liquidated potassium to a glass of perfluorocarbon fluid, mix, and then wait for the remaining undissolved substances to settle, the result can be used to hydrate, however, the solution can only hydrate half as much as normal water. There is a chance of the solution to hydrate better if one adds [[Object 1|Almond Water]].
This part of the description documents notes made by intelligent entities. So far, 184 entity notes have been found throughout the sublevel, only 5 notable ones will be briefly documented here.
'''Archival of the 5 [[Maria]] notes'''
Found notes of [[Maria]] have been mostly localised; and is one of the only instances where [[Maria]] has left more than 2 notes in a level. Notably, Maria is more descriptive in some of these notes, some not having an image at all. {{Quote|Br... it is freezing here... do not drink the water!|Found January 20th 2014}} {{Quote|The darkness is scary. I found none of my friends there, and I never found an exit there... I’m scared...|Found May 12nd 2014}} {{Quote|They look like waterfalls with a flashlight, but it is a door. But it goes back to the lanterns. Many lanterns are good!|Found May 22nd 2015}} {{Quote|These plants are beautiful... but the tunnels are flooded with water... I wish I could take some, but I can’t swim...|Found January 3rd 2016}}{{Quote|The ice is sharp!|Found February 22nd 2021}}
==
Phenomena
== ===
Wet Season
Ball Lightning=== Ball Lightning, taking a replica form of ball lightning in [[The Frontrooms]] is the most common phenomenon in the wet season and is characterised as a small glowing ball that moves erratically through walls or tunnels, and lasts for only a few minutes. Once the ball lightning is burnt out, it makes a loud explosion that can cause temporarily deafness and leaves behind the smell of Sulfur. Like in The Frontrooms, this phenomenon has been unexplained. ===Sudden Bioluminescence Growth Event=== Sudden Bioluminescence Growth Event, S.B.G.E for short is where plants and the level fluid start to grow heavy amounts of bioluminescent plankton, caused by bacteria buildup suddenly mutating into plankton. This plankton is very fast, estimated to move at the fastest 1 meter per minute. The plankton, notably, are known to reproduce 15x more than its Frontrooms counterpart. However, these organisms only can survive for 3 minutes, making these events last 9 to 30 minutes at the most. ===Perfluorocarbon Gas Production=== During the coldest conditions, parts of the leaves might freeze in the biggest plants and start producing slight amounts of Perfluorocarbon Gas, which displaces oxygen, however, due to the gas being anomalously absorbed in by its fluid form, one should not worry about a perfluorocarbon gas outbreak. It should be noted that this phenomenon specifically is known to happen in further regions of the level. ===
Thermotita Season
=== ===Wind Tunnels=== Wind tunnels are wherein consistent hot (29.3-35.1 Celsius) gusts of winds varying from 40 to 67 kph. These wind tunnels can last from 3 minutes to an hour and typically occur further out where plants have grown progressively. This phenomenon is caused when a part of a cave is flooding and starts to rapidly evaporate (through unexplained means), and turns into Helium (rather than perfluorocarbon gas), as it slowly flows the gas starts to create a bond, making the gas molecules to stick together, eventually, this bond will explode causing the gust of air. ===
Dry Season
=== ===Spatial Lakes=== Spatial lakes are an unexplained phenomenon wherein reports of consistent full sensory hallucinations of a huge deep cave lake found near spawn. After manifestation, these lakes disappear in 5 to 10 minutes. People who have said they were in the lake at the time of disappearance, felt a feeling of falling and dampness. Witnesses see the experiencer suddenly unconscious, then afterwards, they wake up with some swollen red spots, saying that "these are from the lake" specifically. === Temporary Neurotransmission Inert State === Arbitrarily, a multitude of neurotransmitters will be either subdued or completely inactive for a temporary amount of time. These periods can last 1 minute to 2 hours. The transmitters that get typically targeted are melatonin, GABA, norepinephrine, and histamine, primarily focused on sleeping and waking systems. If one notices one of the listed below symptoms, resort to physical activities like walking, running, stretching, short rests, and staying near lanterns. * Anxiety * Difficulty in relaxation * Stress * Overthinking * Fatigue * Lack of focus * Drowsiness ==Colonies and Outposts== ===Main Nine Research Field Outpost #32=== The group "Main Nine Research Field" is one of the first successful outposts on this level, being first established in 2008, the outpost serves as a temporary rest hub for wanderers, however, its members live up to the group goal: find/document as much information you can on the main nine levels/sublevels. The group is known for writing a majority of this document and finding harmful effects of the level. The outpost thrives on [[Object 1|Almond Water]] production from Panstuansis and leftover resources from abandoned wanderer bags. The outpost is aiming to sustain a stable lifestyle within the level and to try to make a breakthrough for the group. ===Resource Collecting Center=== From 1999 to 2014, the "Resource Collecting Center", known for supplying many groups, colonies, and bases resources has attempted to stay in '''Level 8.5''' many times throughout the aforementioned period, however has failed due to many environmental and entity danger factors. However, the group has collected an abundance of elemental resources throughout the level. However, due to a lack of fluids and food sources, the group had to leave the level 7 times before fully sustained settlement. The group discovered a majority of the properties and phenomena in '''Level 8.5''', primarily: hydration methods, solar bursts, wind tunnels, magnesium growth in food, and electromagnetic radiation effects. In 2014, the group finally sustained a group, now containing 103 members which have various purposes contributing to survival, science, and backrooms knowledge. So far, some resemblance of education has been made in an attempt for new members, although this education was not sustained due to lack of resources. The group collected over 2000kg of liquid and potassium metal altogether throughout the spontaneous stays.
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Environmental blending in [[Level 8]] eventually leads one into '''Level 8.5'''. *Panic attacks in darker themed levels outside the main nine to [[Level 100]] can have a chance of teleportation into '''Level 8.5'''. *Levels with multiple zones/areas outside the main nine to [[Level 100]] can have a chance of blending the environment into '''Level 8.5''' if one uses an transition point to another zone/area. *Abrupt linear tunnels that have signs in each corner that reads "The story starts where you begin." in [[Level 990]] will transition gradually into '''Level 8.5'''. ===Exits=== *Finding a concrete corridor with metallic doors and entering them may lead one to either: [[Level 193]], [[Level 21]], or [[Level 26]]. *In schizophrenic people, blurring of their sight or passing out may teleport them into either [[Level 8]] or [[Level 9]]. *Finding hard wood or mineshaft wood scattered throughout the floor in a long straight path leads to [[Level 9]]. *Pools of perfluorocarbon liquid that are more then 3 meters deep and swimming to the bottom of them leads them to either [[Level 7]] or [[Level 37.1]]. *Finding frozen perfluorocarbon liquid in big caves or spatial lakes will bring you through the ground and into [[Level 44]]. {{Redirect|Prev=8.2|Next=8.88}}
















{{Collapse|num=428|initial=
i have been exposed to the horror of lies
|final= |body=
Feel the days, its a summer gone by!
Run the tunnels, slowly deprived?
Aren't you crazy? Aren't you bereaved...?
Have you've realized what you've been writing all this time.
A grey monotone prattles. Didn't it talk?
And you walk everywhere you did walk.
Feel anything you want to feel, like:
Happiness, sadness, faith and desire!
Bullshit, pointless, deceiving, another dying fire.
To soon to be lost to the void.
Like everyone else.
I drop the lantern. Then dropped my long clawed hands. My nails hurt, but what is there to care about now…? I contemplated my writing for a few minutes, I wrote that for no reason damnit. So I laid there so I can save my diminished energy. Another apathic tale of the trapped. Each sensory hallucinations made me want to combust. It warred my very self. It was just the loss of focus that comforted me. The time? Well there wasn't time. Would one focus on time if you already know it’s already gone? My friends have likely been lost to the pits of levels. My family might already die to whatever the Frontrooms freely offered them. Fuck my brain. I thought I’d never go through this at all. The straightest path I said, and where did that go...? Huh. I lifted the lantern. It’s very surprising to see that I haven’t taken that water out. "An easy way out." I said to myself. Then laughed a bit thinking to myself. Then I fell from exhaustion and some dream-like senses. Years of inactivity made me feeble. Can’t even get my lazy ass to dig. But I did try years ago. Lately, as I approach an inevitable vegetative stage, the dreamy like sensations were harder and harder to resist. Yes, harder to stay focused, stay grounded to my reality, and sometimes say things that I didn't mean or desire to say. Then I saw the glared light again. Tainted just of the corner of my eye. That was always there since I’ve been here, a light. A yellow dim light always stuck at the corner of my eyes. I can't really notice it but it's been now showing up in one part of the room. And it's been growing brighter and brighter. It was always the brightest of all hallcuna- [[File:Dkwh.png|thumb|center|500px]] No... was it...? Was it a dream? Was it a dream? Was it a dream? Was it a dream? Was it a dream? Was it a dream? Maybe it was.
{{Collapse|num=329758|initial=
i escaped the dream
|final= |body=
"Where am I?" For yes I am right, I have been saved from the horror of lies. A fleeting glowing light emits from the doorway that just appeared in front of me. For once I have been sent hope and sight besides from the lantern. Asleep, no longer. I was sent back to where my story dropped off: [[Level 0]]. The light welcomes me once more, and my shoe stamps onto an endless hard yellow carpet ground, flowing with the carpet fluid I so dearly missed. I wandered for a while ambiguous of anything else in my mind. I am now awake. After a while of continuous pondering, finding seemingly out of place spots with slightly brighter textures, short tight hallways, wrongly faced lights, I questioned them and doubted. Was this false hope? But dismissed those silly... silly questions. Aren't they just apart of the beauty and chaos of the Backrooms...? I entered a hallway which transitioned into [[Level 1]]. This was completely new to me, for I have only heard this level in my... Enter the long car park like chambers. Then I went along the curves, enter the long car park like chambers. Then I went along the curves, enter the long car park like chambers. Then I went along the curves, enter the long car park like chambers. Then I went along the curves. It just got colder?
==The Hope Effect== The Hope Effect manifests as gradual spontaneous periods of hope and decreased awareness and attachment to their surroundings that have been observed in trapped wanderers, or hopeless wanderers usually in response to isolation. If a period lasts longer then 20 minutes, one will hallucinate a white bright glowing door that leads into a mock of [[Level 0]] and [[Level 1]]. Victims in these mocks have knowledge of the main nine and common Backrooms terminology even if not having any recollections on those topics. Notably, they have been observed to be in repetitive and dreamy like states, sharing some form of love and admiration for the levels.
I know where I am. I am awake.
After the first stage, the effect is at his strongest, thoughts and memories are abruptly lost when it comes to thinking anything else other then the level and enters a zombie, robot like state. After stage two, the wanderer becomes more aware, though having unclear previous memories and still retain most delusions. At the same time, the body starts to cool down except the head non-stop, even if anything freezes, the wanderer can still move.
These walls don't seem familiar like I remembered.
In stage four, the final stage, the wanderer starts to become completely aware of the delusions and starts to slowly become frozen. Notably, this stage can only be reached if they come across a dead end within the mock, trapping them and starts to create an entrance back into the actual level. After the wanderer enters back into the level, they enter an endless frozen area. After most of the parts have frozen, the wanderer starts to become ice and smaller. They will be stuck to the ground resembling icicles after they have fully converted. It should be noted that wanderer is conscious even after they have became fully ice.
No. No! NO. NO!

Doesn't each step make you feel colder? I've trusted the very thing I desired. It manipulated me itself. My empty tears faded to the void, sobbing to the frozen perfluorocarbon crystals. The dripping noises fought against silence, even the noises joining my inevitable fate. Each second it was harder to run through the tunnels again. This tunnel went on forever and the ice start to be more apparent. My body ached with drumming, my head is like about to burst every nerve of thought. I couldn't seem to focus. You know you'll be trapped again. Maybe this time for a little bit longer.
}}}}
